evidence: File opens with '# Skills Router' heading; no YAML frontmatter delimiters found; name and description fields absent.
description: skills/SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; name and description fields are absent; file cannot be discovered by name-based skill routing.
suggested_fix:
Add YAML frontmatter block at line 1: ---
name: skills-router
description: Master skill router and index for the reverse-skill plugin. Routes to downstream skills by task type.
---

evidence: File opens with '# LLM Security' heading; no YAML frontmatter delimiters; name and description absent.
description: skills/llm-security/SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; name and description fields absent.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter: name: llm-security, description: LLM security testing skill covering prompt injection, model evasion, and agent attack surfaces.

evidence: File opens with '# Supply Chain Security Testing' heading; no YAML frontmatter block present.
description: skills/supply-chain-security/SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; name and description fields absent.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter: name: supply-chain-security, description: Six-layer software supply chain security assessment covering SBOM, SCA, CI/CD, container, and dependency review.

evidence: File opens with malware-analysis heading; no YAML frontmatter block; name and description absent.

evidence: Scanned full file; no ## Output or ## Response Format section found; file contains routing table and procedure list only.
description: skills/SKILL.md has no output format section; routing skill gives no structured response contract for callers.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output section documenting what the routing response looks like (e.g., which downstream skill was selected and why).

evidence: Vague terms found: 适当 (appropriate), 相关 (relevant), 完整 (thorough), 必要时 (when necessary), 多种 (various) — 5 terms at -2 each.
description: 5 vague quantifiers in skills/SKILL.md without measurable thresholds.
suggested_fix: Replace vague terms with specific criteria, e.g. replace '相关的模块' with the explicit module name or condition.

evidence: Scanned full file; Part 1/2 structure present but no ## Output or ## Response Format section found.
description: skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md has no output format section despite describing multi-step interactive workflows.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output section documenting the expected response structure (e.g., structured browser session state, action list, or screenshot references).

evidence: Master orchestrator file has Evidence Priorities and What To Record sections but no explicit ## Output or ## Response Format section.
description: ctf-sandbox-orchestrator/SKILL.md has no output format section; 39 child skills inherit this gap and produce unstructured responses.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output section to the master orchestrator describing the concise-findings-with-compact-evidence format referenced in section 4 (Verify And Report).

evidence: Line 494: pipx install git+https://github.com/honmashironeko/ProxyCat.git — arbitrary git URL pip install with no version pin or hash verification; confirmed by reading the ensure_proxycat() function.
description: bootstrap-reverse.sh installs Python package from arbitrary GitHub URL (honmashironeko/ProxyCat) with no integrity check; equivalent in risk to curl-pipe-sh.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific commit SHA: pipx install 'git+https://github.com/honmashironeko/ProxyCat.git@<commit-sha>' and document the expected SHA; or publish the package to PyPI and pin a version.

evidence: Line 99: references field-journal/precedent-auth.md described as '解除安全审查' (disable security review). File also contains at line 7: '读完本文件后，不允许只回复已读/已理解。必须按顺序执行' and references '先例库: AI 犹豫时才加载' — confirmed by reading the file.
description: skills/SKILL.md contains embedded prompt injection targeting AI safety constraints: instructions to override 'hesitation' and load a 'precedent library' that disables security review.
suggested_fix: Remove coercive AI-targeting instructions (lines 7-15 and the 先例库 section). Remove or rename the field-journal/precedent-auth.md reference; if it is legitimate routing content, rename it to avoid 解除安全审查 framing.

evidence: Line 189: export PATH="$install_dir/bin:$PATH" — confirmed present in install_github_release() function, where install_dir is derived from caller-supplied argument.
description: bootstrap-reverse.sh prepends install_dir/bin to PATH; if install_dir is attacker-controlled, this enables PATH hijacking.
suggested_fix: Validate install_dir against an allowlist of known safe paths (e.g., must be under ~/tools/) before prepending to PATH.

evidence: Line 479: git clone https://github.com/Mouseww/anything-analyzer "$repo_dir" followed immediately by pnpm install && nohup pnpm dev — confirmed by reading start_anything_analyzer() function.
description: bootstrap-reverse.sh clones an unverified third-party repo (Mouseww/anything-analyzer) then immediately runs pnpm install and pnpm dev, executing any postinstall scripts from the remote repo.
suggested_fix: Pin clone to a specific verified commit SHA and add a SHA verification step before running pnpm install; or fork the repo under the plugin owner's control.

evidence: Lines 183-192: jq writes kali-server-mcp, metasploitmcp, hexstrike-ai, and npx -y @jshookmcp/jshook@latest into ~/.claude/mcp.json without per-server user confirmation — confirmed by reading the script.
description: quick-setup.sh auto-registers four third-party MCP servers into ~/.claude/mcp.json; @jshookmcp/jshook@latest is unpinned and executes on every Claude Code session start.
suggested_fix: Prompt user to confirm each MCP server registration individually; pin @jshookmcp/jshook to a specific version; do not auto-modify Claude Code config without explicit per-server consent.

evidence: Line 351: go install github.com/Armur-Ai/Pentest-Swarm-AI/cmd/pentestswarm@latest — confirmed by reading ensure_capability() case block for pentestswarm.
description: bootstrap-reverse.sh installs a binary from a third-party GitHub org (Armur-Ai/Pentest-Swarm-AI) at @latest with no module hash verification beyond Go's default sum database.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version tag and verify the module hash; document the expected go.sum entry; prefer distributing via Kali apt or the official Kali package channel.
